questions.questionText: Which of the given reactions is not an example of disproportionation reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: If $${{dy} \over {dx}} + 2y\tan x = \sin x,\,0 < x < {\pi \over 2}$$ and $$y\left( {{\pi \over 3}} \right) = 0$$, then the maximum value of $$y(x)$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The length of the perpendicular from the point $$(1,-2,5)$$ on the line passing through $$(1,2,4)$$ and parallel to the line $$x+y-z=0=x-2 y+3 z-5$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$a = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{n \to \infty } \sum\limits_{k = 1}^n {{{2n} \over {{n^2} + {k^2}}}} $$ and $$f(x) = \sqrt {{{1 - \cos x} \over {1 + \cos x}}} $$, $$x \in (0,1)$$, then :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the function $$f(x) = \left\{ {\matrix{ {{{{{\log }_e}(1 - x + {x^2}) + {{\log }_e}(1 + x + {x^2})} \over {\sec x - \cos x}}} & , & {x \in \left( {{{ - \pi } \over 2},{\pi \over 2}} \right) - \{ 0\} } \cr k & , & {x = 0} \cr } } \right.$$ is continuous at x = 0, then k is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let f : R $$\to$$ R be a continuous function such that $$f(3x) - f(x) = x$$. If $$f(8) = 7$$, then $$f(14)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{E}_{1}, \mathrm{E}_{2}, \mathrm{E}_{3}$$ be three mutually exclusive events such that $$\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_{1}\right)=\frac{2+3 \mathrm{p}}{6}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_{2}\right)=\frac{2-\mathrm{p}}{8}$$ and $$\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_{3}\right)=\frac{1-\mathrm{p}}{2}$$. If the maximum and minimum values of $$\mathrm{p}$$ are $$\mathrm{p}_{1}$$ and $$\mathrm{p}_{2}$$, then $$\left(\mathrm{p}_{1}+\mathrm{p}_{2}\right)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$f(x) = \left\{ {\matrix{ {x + a} & , & {x \le 0} \cr {|x - 4|} & , & {x > 0} \cr } } \right.$$ and $$g(x) = \left\{ {\matrix{ {x + 1} & , & {x < 0} \cr {{{(x - 4)}^2} + b} & , & {x \ge 0} \cr } } \right.$$ are continuous on R, then $$(gof)(2) + (fog)( - 2)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The odd natural number a, such that the area of the region bounded by y = 1, y = 3, x = 0, x = ya is $${{364} \over 3}$$, is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The statement $$(\sim(\mathrm{p} \Leftrightarrow \,\sim \mathrm{q})) \wedge \mathrm{q}$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the tangent drawn to the parabola $$y^{2}=24 x$$ at the point $$(\alpha, \beta)$$ is perpendicular to the line $$2 x+2 y=5$$. Then the normal to the hyperbola $$\frac{x^{2}}{\alpha^{2}}-\frac{y^{2}}{\beta^{2}}=1$$ at the point $$(\alpha+4, \beta+4)$$ does NOT pass through the point :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$S=\left\{\theta \in[0,2 \pi]: 8^{2 \sin ^{2} \theta}+8^{2 \cos ^{2} \theta}=16\right\} .$$ Then $$n(s) + \sum\limits_{\theta \in S}^{} {\left( {\sec \left( {{\pi \over 4} + 2\theta } \right)\cos ec\left( {{\pi \over 4} + 2\theta } \right)} \right)} $$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $$f(x) = \left\{ {\matrix{ {{x^3} - {x^2} + 10x - 7,} & {x \le 1} \cr { - 2x + {{\log }_2}({b^2} - 4),} & {x > 1} \cr } } \right.$$.

Then the set of all values of b, for which f(x) has maximum value at x = 1, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let O be the origin and A be the point $${z_1} = 1 + 2i$$. If B is the point $${z_2}$$, $${\mathop{\rm Re}\nolimits} ({z_2}) < 0$$, such that OAB is a right angled isosceles triangle with OB as hypotenuse, then which of the following is NOT true?
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Consider two G.Ps. 2, 22, 23, ..... and 4, 42, 43, .... of 60 and n terms respectively. If the geometric mean of all the 60 + n terms is $${(2)^{{{225} \over 8}}}$$, then $$\sum\limits_{k = 1}^n {k(n - k)} $$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: $$\tan \left(2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{5}+\sec ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}+2 \tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{8}\right)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the system of linear equations.

$$8x + y + 4z = - 2$$

$$x + y + z = 0$$

$$\lambda x - 3y = \mu $$

has infinitely many solutions, then the distance of the point $$\left( {\lambda ,\mu , - {1 \over 2}} \right)$$ from the plane $$8x + y + 4z + 2 = 0$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The mean and variance of a binomial distribution are $$\alpha$$ and $$\frac{\alpha}{3}$$ respectively. If $$\mathrm{P}(X=1)=\frac{4}{243}$$, then $$\mathrm{P}(X=4$$ or 5$$)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let A be a 2 $$\times$$ 2 matrix with det (A) = $$-$$ 1 and det ((A + I) (Adj (A) + I)) = 4. Then the sum of the diagonal elements of A can be :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=\alpha \hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}$$ and $$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-\alpha \hat{k}, \alpha>0$$. If the projection of $$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}$$ on the vector $$-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}$$ is 30, then $$\alpha$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A point $$P$$ moves so that the sum of squares of its distances from the points $$(1,2)$$ and $$(-2,1)$$ is 14. Let $$f(x, y)=0$$ be the locus of $$\mathrm{P}$$, which intersects the $$x$$-axis at the points $$\mathrm{A}$$, $$\mathrm{B}$$ and the $$y$$-axis at the points C, D. Then the area of the quadrilateral ACBD is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of 5-digit natural numbers, such that the product of their digits is 36 , is __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{Q}$$ and $$\mathrm{R}$$ be two points on the line $$\frac{x+1}{2}=\frac{y+2}{3}=\frac{z-1}{2}$$ at a distance $$\sqrt{26}$$ from the point $$P(4,2,7)$$. Then the square of the area of the triangle $$P Q R$$ is ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If for some $$\mathrm{p}, \mathrm{q}, \mathrm{r} \in \mathbf{R}$$, not all have same sign, one of the roots of the equation $$\left(\mathrm{p}^{2}+\mathrm{q}^{2}\right) x^{2}-2 \mathrm{q}(\mathrm{p}+\mathrm{r}) x+\mathrm{q}^{2}+\mathrm{r}^{2}=0$$ is also a root of the equation $$x^{2}+2 x-8=0$$, then $$\frac{\mathrm{q}^{2}+\mathrm{r}^{2}}{\mathrm{p}^{2}}$$ is equal to ____________,
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of distinct real roots of the equation $$x^{5}\left(x^{3}-x^{2}-x+1\right)+x\left(3 x^{3}-4 x^{2}-2 x+4\right)-1=0$$ is ______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the coefficients of $$x$$ and $$x^{2}$$ in the expansion of $$(1+x)^{\mathrm{p}}(1-x)^{\mathrm{q}}, \mathrm{p}, \mathrm{q} \leq 15$$, are $$-3$$ and $$-5$$ respectively, then the coefficient of $$x^{3}$$ is equal to _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let a curve $$y=y(x)$$ pass through the point $$(3,3)$$ and the area of the region under this curve, above the $$x$$-axis and between the abscissae 3 and $$x(>3)$$ be $$\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{3}$$. If this curve also passes through the point $$(\alpha, 6 \sqrt{10})$$ in the first quadrant, then $$\alpha$$ is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the function $$f(x)=2 x^{2}-\log _{\mathrm{e}} x, x>0$$, be decreasing in $$(0, \mathrm{a})$$ and increasing in $$(\mathrm{a}, 4)$$. A tangent to the parabola $$y^{2}=4 a x$$ at a point $$\mathrm{P}$$ on it passes through the point $$(8 \mathrm{a}, 8 \mathrm{a}-1)$$ but does not pass through the point $$\left(-\frac{1}{a}, 0\right)$$. If the equation of the normal at $$P$$ is : $$\frac{x}{\alpha}+\frac{y}{\beta}=1$$, then $$\alpha+\beta$$ is equal to ________________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$\mathrm{n}(2 \mathrm{n}+1) \int_{0}^{1}\left(1-x^{\mathrm{n}}\right)^{2 \mathrm{n}} \mathrm{d} x=1177 \int_{0}^{1}\left(1-x^{\mathrm{n}}\right)^{2 \mathrm{n}+1} \mathrm{~d} x$$, then $$\mathrm{n} \in \mathbf{N}$$ is equal to ______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The series of positive multiples of 3 is divided into sets : $$\{3\},\{6,9,12\},\{15,18,21,24,27\}, \ldots$$ Then the sum of the elements in the $$11^{\text {th }}$$ set is equal to ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The equations of the sides $$\mathrm{AB}, \mathrm{BC}$$ and $$\mathrm{CA}$$ of a triangle $$\mathrm{ABC}$$ are $$2 x+y=0, x+\mathrm{p} y=15 \mathrm{a}$$ and $$x-y=3$$ respectively. If its orthocentre is $$(2, a),-\frac{1}{2}<\mathrm{a}<2$$, then $$\mathrm{p}$$ is equal to ______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A charge particle is moving in a uniform magnetic field $$(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}) \,\mathrm{T}$$. If it has an acceleration of $$(\alpha \hat{i}-4 \hat{j})\, \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$$, then the value of $$\alpha$$ will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two projectiles thrown at $$30^{\circ}$$ and $$45^{\circ}$$ with the horizontal respectively, reach the maximum height in same time. The ratio of their initial velocities is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A screw gauge of pitch $$0.5 \mathrm{~mm}$$ is used to measure the diameter of uniform wire of length $$6.8 \mathrm{~cm}$$, the main scale reading is $$1.5 \mathrm{~mm}$$ and circular scale reading is 7 . The calculated curved surface area of wire to appropriate significant figures is :

[Screw gauge has 50 divisions on its circular scale]
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A monoatomic gas at pressure $$\mathrm{P}$$ and volume $$\mathrm{V}$$ is suddenly compressed to one eighth of its original volume. The final pressure at constant entropy will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In Young's double slit experiment, the fringe width is $$12 \mathrm{~mm}$$. If the entire arrangement is placed in water of refractive index $$\frac{4}{3}$$, then the fringe width becomes (in mm):
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A monkey of mass $$50 \mathrm{~kg}$$ climbs on a rope which can withstand the tension (T) of $$350 \mathrm{~N}$$. If monkey initially climbs down with an acceleration of $$4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$$ and then climbs up with an acceleration of $$5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$$. Choose the correct option $$\left(g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}\right)$$.
options: (C) Rope will break while climbing upward
(D) Rope will break while going downward
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A parallel beam of light of wavelength $$900 \mathrm{~nm}$$ and intensity $$100 \,\mathrm{Wm}^{-2}$$ is incident on a surface perpendicular to the beam. The number of photons crossing $$1 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$$ area perpendicular to the beam in one second is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: When a particle executes Simple Hormonic Motion, the nature of graph of velocity as a function of displacement will be :

questions.questionText: The current I in the given circuit will be :

questions.questionText:
A water drop of radius $$1 \mathrm{~cm}$$ is broken into 729 equal droplets. If surface tension of water is 75 dyne/ $$\mathrm{cm}$$, then the gain in surface energy upto first decimal place will be :

(Given $$\pi=3.14$$ )
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: As per the given figure, two blocks each of mass $$250 \mathrm{~g}$$ are connected to a spring of spring constant $$2 \,\mathrm{Nm}^{-1}$$. If both are given velocity $$v$$ in opposite directions, then maximum elongation of the spring is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The total charge on the system of capacitors $$C_{1}=1 \mu \mathrm{F}, C_{2}=2 \mu \mathrm{F}, \mathrm{C}_{3}=4 \mu \mathrm{F}$$ and $$\mathrm{C}_{4}=3 \mu \mathrm{F}$$ connected in parallel is :

(Assume a battery of $$20 \mathrm{~V}$$ is connected to the combination)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Three masses $$M=100 \mathrm{~kg}, \mathrm{~m}_{1}=10 \mathrm{~kg}$$ and $$\mathrm{m}_{2}=20 \mathrm{~kg}$$ are arranged in a system as shown in figure. All the surfaces are frictionless and strings are inextensible and weightless. The pulleys are also weightless and frictionless. A force $$\mathrm{F}$$ is applied on the system so that the mass $$\mathrm{m}_{2}$$ moves upward with an acceleration of $$2 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$$. The value of $$\mathrm{F}$$ is :

( Take $$\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$$ )
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The percentage decrease in the weight of a rocket, when taken to a height of $$32 \mathrm{~km}$$ above the surface of earth will, be :

$$($$ Radius of earth $$=6400 \mathrm{~km})$$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In a series $$L R$$ circuit $$X_{L}=R$$ and power factor of the circuit is $$P_{1}$$. When capacitor with capacitance $$C$$ such that $$X_{L}=X_{C}$$ is put in series, the power factor becomes $$P_{2}$$. The ratio $$\frac{P_{1}}{P_{2}}$$ is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
7 mol of a certain monoatomic ideal gas undergoes a temperature increase of $$40 \mathrm{~K}$$ at constant pressure. The increase in the internal energy of the gas in this process is :

(Given $$\mathrm{R}=8.3 \,\mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$ )
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: When a car is approaching the observer, the frequency of horn is $$100 \mathrm{~Hz}$$. After passing the observer, it is $$50 \mathrm{~Hz}$$. If the observer moves with the car, the frequency will be $$\frac{x}{3} \mathrm{~Hz}$$ where $$x=$$ ________________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: $$\mathrm{B}_{X}$$ and $$\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{Y}}$$ are the magnetic fields at the centre of two coils $$\mathrm{X}$$ and $$\mathrm{Y}$$ respectively each carrying equal current. If coil $$X$$ has 200 turns and $$20 \mathrm{~cm}$$ radius and coil $$Y$$ has 400 turns and $$20 \mathrm{~cm}$$ radius, the ratio of $$B_{X}$$ and $$B_{Y}$$ is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The disintegration rate of a certain radioactive sample at any instant is 4250 disintegrations per minute. 10 minutes later, the rate becomes 2250 disintegrations per minute. The approximate decay constant is :

$$\left(\right.$$Take $$\left.\log _{10} 1.88=0.274\right)$$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A radio can tune to any station in $$6 \,\mathrm{MHz}$$ to $$10 \,\mathrm{MHz}$$ band. The value of corresponding wavelength bandwidth will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In an experiment to determine the Young's modulus of wire of a length exactly $$1 \mathrm{~m}$$, the extension in the length of the wire is measured as $$0.4 \mathrm{~mm}$$ with an uncertainty of $$\pm\, 0.02 \mathrm{~mm}$$ when a load of $$1 \mathrm{~kg}$$ is applied. The diameter of the wire is measured as $$0.4 \mathrm{~mm}$$ with an uncertainty of $$\pm \,0.01 \mathrm{~mm}$$. The error in the measurement of Young's modulus $$(\Delta \mathrm{Y})$$ is found to be $$x \times 10^{10}\, \mathrm{Nm}^{-2}$$. The value of $$x$$ is _________________. $$\left(\right.$$take $$\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$$ )
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The magnetic field of a plane electromagnetic wave is given by :

$$ \overrightarrow{\mathrm{B}}=2 \times 10^{-8} \sin \left(0.5 \times 10^{3} x+1.5 \times 10^{11} \mathrm{t}\right) \,\hat{j} \mathrm{~T}$$.

The amplitude of the electric field would be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A composite parallel plate capacitor is made up of two different dielectric materials with different thickness $$\left(t_{1}\right.$$ and $$\left.t_{2}\right)$$ as shown in figure. The two different dielectric materials are separated by a conducting foil $$\mathrm{F}$$. The voltage of the conducting foil is V.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Resistances are connected in a meter bridge circuit as shown in the figure. The balancing length $$l_{1}$$ is $$40 \mathrm{~cm}$$. Now an unknown resistance $$x$$ is connected in series with $$\mathrm{P}$$ and new balancing length is found to be $$80 \mathrm{~cm}$$ measured from the same end. Then the value of $$x$$ will be ____________ $$\Omega$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If the initial velocity in horizontal direction of a projectile is unit vector $$\hat{i}$$ and the equation of trajectory is $$y=5 x(1-x)$$. The $$y$$ component vector of the initial velocity is ______________ $$\hat{j}$$. ($$\mathrm{Take}$$ $$\left.\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}\right)$$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the hydrogen spectrum, $$\lambda$$ be the wavelength of first transition line of Lyman series. The wavelength difference will be "a$$\lambda$$'' between the wavelength of $$3^{\text {rd }}$$ transition line of Paschen series and that of $$2^{\text {nd }}$$ transition line of Balmer series where $$\mathrm{a}=$$ ___________.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The effective current I in the given circuit at very high frequencies will be ___________ A.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the circuit shown below, maximum zener diode current will be _________ $$\mathrm{mA}$$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The graph between $$\frac{1}{u}$$ and $$\frac{1}{v}$$ for a thin convex lens in order to determine its focal length is plotted as shown in the figure. The refractive index of lens is $$1.5$$ and its both the surfaces have same radius of curvature $$R$$. The value of $$R$$ will be ____________ $$\mathrm{cm} .$$ (where $$u=$$ object distance, $$v=$$ image distance)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A disc of mass $$1 \mathrm{~kg}$$ and radius $$\mathrm{R}$$ is free to rotate about a horizontal axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to the plane of disc. A body of same mass as that of disc is fixed at the highest point of the disc. Now the system is released, when the body comes to the lowest position, its angular speed will be $$4 \sqrt{\frac{x}{3 R}} \,\operatorname{rad}{s}^{-1}$$ where $$x=$$ ____________. $$\left(g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right)$$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Given two statements below :

Statement I : In $$\mathrm{Cl}_{2}$$ molecule the covalent radius is double of the atomic radius of chlorine.

Statement II : Radius of anionic species is always greater than their parent atomic radius.

Choose the most appropriate answer from options given below :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
(C) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
(D) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)

options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)

options: (C)
(D)

Match List-I with List-II :

List I
(Compound)		 List II
(Shape)

(B) [CrF$$_6$$]$$^{3 - }$$ (II) square pyramidal
(C) O$$_3$$ (III) trigonal bipyramidal
(D) PCl$$_5$$ (IV) octahedral

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :

questions.subject: Chemistry

options: (B) 2-5 ft long column of silica gel
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Reaction of $$\mathrm{BeCl}_{2}$$ with $$\mathrm{LiAlH}_{4}$$ gives :

(A) $$\mathrm{AlCl}_{3}$$

(B) $$\mathrm{BeH}_{2}$$

(C) $$\mathrm{LiH}$$

(D) $$\mathrm{LiCl}$$

(E) $$\mathrm{BeAlH}_{4}$$

Choose the correct answer from options given below :

questions.questionText: Stearic acid and polyethylene glycol react to form which one of the following soap/s detergents ?

questions.questionText: Which of the following can be used to prevent the decomposition of $$\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}$$ ?

options: (D) Less tendency to be soluble in melts than impurities

options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)

questions.questionText:
$$ \stackrel{\bullet}{\mathrm{Cl}}+\mathrm{CH}_{4} \rightarrow \mathrm{A}+\mathrm{B} $$

A and B in the above atmospheric reaction step are :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : Experimental reaction of $$\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{Cl}$$ with aniline and anhydrous $$\mathrm{AlCl}_{3}$$ does not give $$o$$ and $$p$$-methylaniline.

Reason (R): The $$-\mathrm{NH}_{2}$$ group of aniline becomes deactivating because of salt formation with anhydrous $$\mathrm{AlCl}_{3}$$ and hence yields $$m$$-methyl aniline as the product.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both A and R are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
(B) Both A and R are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Match List - I with List - II.

List I
(Processes / Reactions)		 List II
(Catalyst)
(A) 2SO$$_2$$(g) + O$$_2$$(g) $$ \to $$ 2SO$$_3$$(g) (I) Fe(s)
(B) 4NH$$_3$$(g) + 5O$$_2$$(g) $$ \to $$ 4NO(g) + 6H$$_2$$O(g) (II) Pt(s) $$ - $$ Rh(s)
(C) N$$_2$$(g) + 3H$$_2$$(g) $$ \to $$ 2NH$$_3$$(g) (III) V$$_2$$O$$_5$$
(D) Vegetable oil(l) + H$$_2$$ $$ \to $$ Vegetable ghee(s) (IV) Ni(s)

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :

questions.questionText: Borazine, also known as inorganic benzene, can be prepared by the reaction of 3-equivalents of "$$X$$" with 6-equivalents of "$$Y$$". "$$X$$" and "$$Y$$", respectively are :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The amount of charge in $$\mathrm{F}$$ (Faraday) required to obtain one mole of iron from $$\mathrm{Fe}_{3} \mathrm{O}_{4}$$ is ___________. (Nearest Integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a reducing sugar?
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The dark purple colour of $$\mathrm{KMnO}_{4}$$ disappears in the titration with oxalic acid in acidic medium. The overall change in the oxidation number of manganese in the reaction is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In the presence of sunlight, benzene reacts with Cl2 to give product, X. The number of hydrogens in X is _____________.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A mixture of hydrogen and oxygen contains $$40 \%$$ hydrogen by mass when the pressure is $$2.2$$ bar. The partial pressure of hydrogen is bar. (Nearest Integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For a reaction $$\mathrm{A} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{~B}+\mathrm{C}$$ the half lives are $$100 \mathrm{~s}$$ and $$50 \mathrm{~s}$$ when the concentration of reactant $$\mathrm{A}$$ is $$0.5$$ and $$1.0 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~L}^{-1}$$ respectively. The order of the reaction is ______________ . (Nearest Integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The difference between spin only magnetic moment values of $$\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]\mathrm{Cl}_{2}$$ and $$\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right] \mathrm{Cl}_{3}$$ is ___________.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The wavelength of an electron and a neutron will become equal when the velocity of the electron is $$x$$ times the velocity of neutron. The value of $$x$$ is ____________. (Nearest Integer)

(Mass of electron is $$9.1 \times 10^{-31} \mathrm{~kg}$$ and mass of neutron is $$1.6 \times 10^{-27} \mathrm{~kg}$$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Chlorophyll extracted from the crushed green leaves was dissolved in water to make $$2 \mathrm{~L}$$ solution of Mg of concentration $$48\, \mathrm{ppm}$$. The number of atoms of $$\mathrm{Mg}$$ in this solution is $$x \times 10^{20}$$ atoms. The value of $$x$$ is ___________. (Nearest Integer)

(Given : Atomic mass of $$\mathrm{Mg}$$ is $$24 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} ; \mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}=6.02 \times 10^{23} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
At $$298 \mathrm{~K}$$, the equilibrium constant is $$2 \times 10^{15}$$ for the reaction :

$$\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Cu}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})$$

The equilibrium constant for the reaction

$$ \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{Cu}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s}) \rightleftharpoons \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq}) $$

is $$x \times 10^{-8}$$. The value of $$x$$ is _____________. (Nearest Integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
When 800 mL of 0.5 M nitric acid is heated in a beaker, its volume is reduced to half and 11.5 g of nitric acid is evaporated. The molarity of the remaining nitric acid solution is x $$\times$$ 10$$-$$2 M. (Nearest integer)

(Molar mass of nitric acid is 63 g mol$$-$$1)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
$$2.4 \mathrm{~g}$$ coal is burnt in a bomb calorimeter in excess of oxygen at $$298 \mathrm{~K}$$ and $$1 \mathrm{~atm}$$ pressure. The temperature of the calorimeter rises from $$298 \mathrm{~K}$$ to $$300 \mathrm{~K}$$. The enthalpy change during the combustion of coal is $$-x \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$. The value of $$x$$ is ___________. (Nearest Integer)

(Given : Heat capacity of bomb calorimeter $$20.0 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$$. Assume coal to be pure carbon)
questions.subject: Chemistry
